name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
summary: Marjorie Taylor Greene has consistently demonstrated a pattern of behavior that promotes conspiracy theories, bigotry, and violence. She has supported and amplified white supremacist, Islamophobic, and antisemitic views, and has called for the execution of prominent Democratic politicians. Greene has also spread misinformation about COVID-19, vaccines, and public health measures, and has voted against bills that promote equality and human rights. Her actions and rhetoric have been marked by a disregard for facts, a lack of empathy, and a willingness to incite violence and hatred. Greene's behavior has been so extreme that she was expelled from the House Freedom Caucus and removed from committee roles, and has been fined multiple times for violating House rules.

entries.fact: Marjorie Taylor Greene began writing for the now defunct American Truth Seekers, a conspiracy news website, which published 59 articles by Greene
Marjorie Taylor Greene started writing for Law Enforcement Today, a pro-police fake news website, which featured 27 articles Greene authored
Marjorie Taylor Greene was a top official of the Family America Project, a conservative group, where members posted death threats against Democrats and bigotry directed at the family of Barack Obama

Marjorie Taylor Greene visited the U.S. Capitol and congressional offices, where she posted livestream videos outside Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's office, calling through the mail slot for Ocasio-Cortez to unlock the door and 'face the American citizens that you serve'
Marjorie Taylor Greene referred to Ocasio-Cortez's office as a day care center in a livestream video
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that representatives Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib were not 'official' representatives because they were sworn in to Congress on the Quran
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that she wanted Omar and Tlaib to instead be sworn in on the Bible and accused the two representatives of supporting Islamic law
Marjorie Taylor Greene supported calls to execute prominent Democratic Party politicians, including Hillary Clinton and Barack Obama
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Islamophobic, antisemitic, and white supremacist views including the white genocide conspiracy theory

Marjorie Taylor Greene amplified conspiracy theories that allege government involvement in mass shootings in the United States, implicate the Clinton family in murder, and suggest the attacks of 9/11 were a hoax

Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a gun rights rally in Villanow hosted by Silent No Longer, a group campaigning to make Walker County a Second Amendment sanctuary, and posed for photos with Chester Doles, a 5th-generation Ku Klux Klansman
Marjorie Taylor Greene held an AR-15 style rifle in a video and warned antifa groups to 'stay the hell out of Northwest Georgia', which was taken down by Facebook for violating its terms of service

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a meme depicting herself holding an AR-15 style rifle next to a collage of pictures of Ocasio-Cortez, Omar, and Tlaib, with the caption 'Squad's worst nightmare', which was deleted by Facebook for violating its policies on inciting violence
Greene called Nancy Pelosi 'anti-American', a 'hypocrite', and a 'bitch' during her victory speech
Greene said 'we're going to kick [Pelosi] out of Congress' during her victory speech
Greene made deceptive claims that her husband Perry's Floyd County voting record revealed voter fraud
Marjorie Taylor Greene equated the Democratic Party with Nazis, and compared COVID-19 safety measures to the persecution of Jews during the Holocaust
Greene wore a face mask onto the House floor that read 'Trump Won', despite Trump having lost the 2020 U.S. presidential election
Greene raised an objection to counting Michigan's electoral votes during the counting of electoral votes
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a face mask while sheltering in place during the riot at the US Capitol
The U.S. House of Representatives voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from all committee roles in response to her endorsements of political violence
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Russian propaganda and praised its president Vladimir Putin during the Russian invasion of Ukraine
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the conservative House Freedom Caucus after insulting fellow caucus member Congresswoman Lauren Boebert
Marjorie Taylor Greene unsuccessfully attempted to oust Mike Johnson from his role as Speaker of the House of Representatives

Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote on Twitter that children should not wear masks, calling recommendations by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and other public health officials 'unhealthy for their psychological, emotional, and educational growth' and 'emasculating' for boys.
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a mask in a secured room with other members of Congress during the storming of the Capitol.
Greene tweeted that Democrats must be held accountable for the political violence inspired by their rhetoric
Greene falsely suggested that the rioters were members of antifa dressed as Trump supporters
Greene filed articles of impeachment against President Joe Biden, citing alleged abuse of power

Marjorie Taylor Greene and Lauren Boebert cast two lone votes against a bill to reauthorize the National Marrow Donor Program, with Greene falsely claiming that the bill could fund aborted fetal tissue.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the Plan B contraceptive 'kills a baby in the womb', when in fact it prevents ovulation and thus prevents pregnancy.
Greene voted against the January 6 commission, a proposed commission which would have investigated the attack on the Capitol
Greene called for justice for Ashli Babbitt, a Trump supporter who was fatally shot by a Capitol police officer when she stormed the Capitol and attempted to break into the House chamber
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined $500 for not wearing a mask on the House floor.

Marjorie Taylor Greene called Nancy Pelosi 'mentally ill' and compared the requirement to wear masks to the Holocaust, saying it was 'exactly the type of abuse' as Jews being 'put in trains and taken to gas chambers in Nazi Germany'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined $2,500 for again entering the House floor without a mask.
Marjorie Taylor Greene defended her comments, adding, 'any rational Jewish person doesn't like what's happening with overbearing mask mandates and overbearing vaccine policies'
Greene was one of 21 House Republicans to vote against a resolution to give the Congressional Gold Medal to police officers who defended the U.S. Capitol on January 6
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Biden's suggestion to provide door-to-door vaccinations to 'medical brownshirts' and said: 'You can't force people to be part of the human experiment'
Marjorie Taylor Greene promised that companies that complied with the House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack's request to retain phone records would be 'shut down'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a conference hosted by white supremacist Nick Fuentes, at which Fuentes called the January 6 attack 'awesome' and praised Putin and Hitler.
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced criticisms of her attendance at the white supremacist conference as 'identity politics' and an attempt to 'cancel' her, and labelled critics 'Pharisees'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the Freedom Caucus by a vote of its members after she called fellow caucus member Lauren Boebert a 'little bitch' during an argument on the House floor.
Marjorie Taylor Greene and Congresswoman Elise Stefanik introduced a pair of resolutions to expunge both of Trump's impeachments.
Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized Trump's support for Israeli strikes against Iran and opposed the possible involvement of the United States in the war.

Marjorie Taylor Greene began supporting the House of Representatives' discharge petition that would release the files from the Jeffrey Epstein client list and criticized Trump for actively opposing the database's release.

Greene's Facebook account responded to a post asking about hanging leading Democrats, saying "Stage is being set. Players are being put in place. We must be patient. This must be done perfectly or liberal judges would let them off."
Marjorie Taylor Greene confronted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg near the U.S. Capitol, questioning his gun control activism and calling him a 'coward' for ignoring her
Marjorie Taylor Greene said she would always protect gun owners' rights and would not vote for any laws making it harder for people to possess guns at a gun rights rally in Ringgold, Georgia
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a measure condemning the Myanmar coup d'état
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a bill to abolish the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted to repeal the Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq Resolution of 2002
Marjorie Taylor Greene sent Biden a letter calling for an investigation into Fauci over his statements on the origin of SARS-CoV-2, the virus that causes COVID-19
Marjorie Taylor Greene called COVID-19 a 'manufactured plague' caused by a 'Chinese-made virus'
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that only a bioweapon could explain the existence and spread of the virus
Marjorie Taylor Greene dismissed the COVID-19 variants, including the Delta variant, saying, 'no one cares'
Marjorie Taylor Greene posted misinformation about the virus, saying it 'is not dangerous for non-obese people and those under 65'
Marjorie Taylor Greene stated she would kick out every Chinese person in the United States who is loyal to the Chinese Communist Party and impose strict tariffs on China if she had the authority
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the bipartisan ALLIES Act, which would increase the number of special immigrant visas for Afghan allies of the U.S. military
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Biden would be 'sending one of his police state friends to your front door' to inquire whether they had received a vaccine

Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely said that hospitals were not overwhelmed with afflicted patients, and added, 'we're human, we can't live forever, we're going to catch all kinds of diseases and illnesses and other viruses'
Marjorie Taylor Greene had been fined 20 times for not wearing a mask on the House floor, totaling $48,000 in fines
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that any Republican who supported the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act was 'a traitor to our party, a traitor to their voters and a traitor to our donors'
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the $1.2 trillion Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act Biden's 'communist takeover of America'
Marjorie Taylor Greene argued that Canada's proposed gun control laws would leave Canada 'weak and vulnerable to being invaded and easily taken over by another stronger country'

Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the July 4 Highland Park parade shooting was 'designed to make Republicans go along with more gun control' because the shooting occurred 'in a rich, white neighborhood'
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the anti-vaccine film Died Suddenly, which promotes misinformation about COVID-19 vaccines and Great Reset conspiracy theories
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted in favor of H.Con.Res. 21, which directed President Biden to remove U.S. troops from Syria within 180 days
Marjorie Taylor Greene sponsored a resolution to censure Democratic U.S. representative Rashida Tlaib for alleged 'antisemitic activity' and 'sympathizing' with Hamas
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed an amendment to a House foreign aid bill on Ukraine that would require all members of the House voting in favor of the bill to enlist in the Ukrainian military
Marjorie Taylor Greene called for the lifting of U.S. sanctions on Russia
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 18 Republicans to vote against admitting Sweden and Finland to NATO
Marjorie Taylor Greene repeatedly refused to address Fauci as Doctor, questioned his medical licence and accused him of covering up COVID-19's origins during a congressional hearing
Marjorie Taylor Greene became the first Republican to call the situation in Gaza a 'genocide'
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed a defense appropriations amendment that would end U.S. funding of Israel's Iron Dome
Marjorie Taylor Greene broke with her party over the federal government shutdown due to a proposed extension of Obamacare subsidies
Greene speculated that the Pizzagate conspiracy theory is real
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2017 Charlottesville white nationalist rally was an 'inside job'
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed doubt that the perpetrator of the 2017 Las Vegas shooting acted alone, suggesting it was an attack on the right to bear arms
Marjorie Taylor Greene said Nancy Pelosi is guilty of treason and that treason is a crime punishable by death in a Facebook video
Greene suggested that Nancy Pelosi would either be killed or imprisoned for treason in Facebook live-streamed videos
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Black Lives Matter activists to white nationalist participants at the August 2017 Unite the Right rally in Charlottesville, Virginia.
Marjorie Taylor Greene told viewers that 'the only way you get your freedoms back is it's earned with the price of blood' in an interview with gun activist Chris Dorr
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Muslims who believe in Sharia law should not be in the U.S. government and contended that the Democratic Party is holding Black Americans as 'slaves'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene referred to the Squad as the 'Jihad Squad' and said they 'don't belong in Congress'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene had support for and past ties with extremist militia groups, including the Three Percenters and the Oath Keepers
Marjorie Taylor Greene co-sponsored the Old Glory Only Bill, a bill to ban U.S. embassies from flying pride flags
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the Equality Act would 'destroy God's creation' and 'violate everything we hold dear in God's creation'

Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed replacing the Equality Act with a bill that would exempt nonprofit organizations, allow people to sue the federal government 'if their religious rights are violated', and prevent trans women and girls from participating in women's sports
Marjorie Taylor Greene displayed a transphobic poster outside her office reading: 'There are TWO genders: Male & Female. Trust The Science!'

Marjorie Taylor Greene supported a bill by Representative Mary Miller that would bar schools from allowing transgender students to use facilities such as bathrooms and locker rooms that correspond to their gender identity

A leaked document stated the America First caucus, which Marjorie Taylor Greene was involved with, believed 'America is a nation with a border, and a culture, strengthened by a common respect for uniquely Anglo-Saxon political traditions'

Marjorie Taylor Greene said the verdict in the Derek Chauvin trial was a result of jury intimidation by Black Lives Matter and falsely asserted that Washington, D.C. was 'completely dead' on the night of the verdict.
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act, a civil rights and police reform bill, as an 'Anti-Police bill'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called transgender women 'men playing dress up' and 'literally having an identity crisis', saying, 'They never will be [women], and I refuse to recognize them that way'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that schools teaching about LGBT people is 'mental/emotional child abuse'
Marjorie Taylor Greene advocated a 'national divorce' between red states and blue states, suggesting that red states disenfranchise people who move there from blue states for a period of five years
Marjorie Taylor Greene attended the America First Political Action Conference, which was criticized by Senate minority leader Mitch McConnell for having 'white supremacists or anti-Semitism'
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that straight people would go extinct within less than two centuries due to LGBT-inclusive educators, calling them 'trans terrorists'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that 'an entire' pride month celebrating LGBT people 'needs to end'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'People die in the cold. This Earth warming, and carbon, is actually healthy for us. It helps us to feed people, it keeps people alive' in an interview, contrary to the scientific consensus on climate change
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced the 'Protect Children's Innocence Act', which would make giving gender-affirming care to transgender youth a felony punishable by up to 25 years in prison.
Marjorie Taylor Greene responded to California state senator Scott Wiener by saying that the Protect Children's Innocence Act would stop 'communist groomers' such as Wiener.
Marjorie Taylor Greene repeated her suggestion of a 'national divorce' between red states and blue states, to the condemnation of Democrats and some Republicans

Marjorie Taylor Greene posted online that 'Yes they can control the weather … It's ridiculous for anyone to lie and say it can't be done' after Hurricane Helene caused destruction in several American states
An updated version of Marjorie Taylor Greene's 'Protect Children's Innocence Act' passed the House by a vote of 216–211.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that 'many of the things' said on 4chan by the eponymous Q — whom she called a 'patriot' — 'have really proven to be true'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said, 'There's a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to take this global cabal of Satan-worshipping pedophiles out, and I think we have the president to do it'
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed support for a conspiracy theory that a plane did not hit the Pentagon during the September 11 attacks
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2018 Stoneman Douglas High School shooting in Parkland, Florida, was an organized false flag operation
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a false flag operation

Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote that 'I am told that Nancy Pelosi tells Hillary Clinton several times a month that 'we need another school shooting' in order to persuade the public to want strict gun control'
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested John F. Kennedy Jr.'s death in a plane crash in 1999 was a "Clinton murder" because he was a possible rival to her for a U.S. Senate election in New York
Greene shared a video promoting the antisemitic white genocide conspiracy theory that Zionists are conspiring to flood Europe with migrants to replace the native white populations.

Greene shared a conspiracy theory about the Camp Fire, a deadly Californian wildfire, suggesting that it could have been caused by "what looked like lasers or beams of blue light" from "space solar generators" in a scheme involving California governor Jerry Brown, companies PG&E, Rothschild & Co, and Solaren.
Marjorie Taylor Greene indicated support for executing prominent Democrats
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg had been replaced in public appearances by a body double
Marjorie Taylor Greene followed and taunted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg, accusing him of using children for his cause
Marjorie Taylor Greene live-streamed a video targeting Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg, calling him a "coward" and falsely claiming he is funded by George Soros.
Greene expressed support for the execution of leading Democrats, including Pelosi, Barack Obama, and Hillary Clinton, as well as support for the execution of FBI agents on her Facebook account
Greene published videos expressing racist, antisemitic, and Islamophobic views
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg '#littleHitler'

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops, accusing them of 'destroying' America with their work regarding immigrants and refugees and claiming that Satan was 'controlling the church'.
Greene promoted a conspiracy theory that the U.S. government is planning to force Americans to eat fake meat grown in a "peach tree dish" by Bill Gates
Greene said that the Robb Elementary School shooter had engaged in cross-dressing, echoing similar claims by right-wing politicians and media figures that the shooter was transgender.

Greene claimed without evidence that the Robb Elementary School shooter was in the same Discord server as the teen accused of carrying out the 2022 Buffalo shooting and that there was a third party who "groomed" both young men.

Greene criticized the presence of the Georgia Guidestones, a megalithic granite monument, saying its ecumenical texts represented a nefarious future of "population control" as envisioned by the "hard left".
Greene said that Democrats had designed the killing of Ayman al-Zawahiri to boost Biden in the midterms
